Title: Red colouration in apple fruit is due to the activity of the MYB transcription factor, MdMYB10

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Red Field; Pacific RoseTM; Granny Smith
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Red Field; Pacific RoseTM; Royal Gala; Samsun
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Apple fruit cortex; apple fruit skin; apple leaves in transgenics; tobacco leaf epidermal cell vacuoles
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Red pigmentation/colouration of apple fruit and other tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and red colouration in apple tissues; sufficient to induce anthocyanin accumulation in tobacco leaves and highly pigmented transgenic apple
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Subgroup 10 R2R3-MYB; anthocyanin regulator-like MYB with bHLH interaction motif
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary determinant/limiting regulator controlling anthocyanin accumulation and flesh/skin colour in apple
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR promoter; anthocyanin biosynthetic genes including CHS; CHI; F3H; DFR1; LDOX; UFGT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates DFR promoter with stronger activation in presence of apple bHLH cofactors; expression strongly correlates with elevated anthocyanin biosynthetic gene transcripts, especially UFGT
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental stage; tissue type; likely environmental cues associated with colour development
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with apple bHLH partners MdbHLH3 and MdbHLH33; discussed within MYB-bHLH-WD40 regulatory complex framework
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in red-fleshed 'Red Field' fruit cortex and skin, especially 40, 102, 130 and 146 DAFB; barely detectable in Pacific RoseTM cortex; detectable in Pacific RoseTM skin at 102 DAFB; also expressed in Red Field leaf
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In apple, high in pigmented tissues during fruit development; constitutive 35S-driven overexpression in transgenic Royal Gala; transiently expressed in tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; transient Agrobacterium infiltration; constitutive 35S overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR expression profiling across development; phylogenetic analysis; dual-luciferase promoter activation assay; transient tobacco infiltration colour assay; HPLC/LC-MS anthocyanin profiling; stable 35S overexpression in transgenic apple
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB10 transcript abundance strongly correlates with anthocyanin accumulation in red-fleshed apple cortex and pigmented skin during fruit development. 2. MdMYB10 activates anthocyanin biosynthesis in heterologous tobacco and homologous apple systems, causing visible red pigmentation and anthocyanin accumulation. 3. MdMYB10 activates DFR promoter activity most strongly when co-expressed with apple bHLH cofactors, indicating MYB-bHLH-dependent regulation. 4. Differences in apple colour are attributed mainly to differences in MdMYB10 expression rather than coding-sequence differences.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for breeding or engineering red-fleshed/high-anthocyanin apples; can be used as marker/causal gene for colour selection and as transgene or expression-modulation target, with possible benefit from stacking with compatible bHLH cofactors
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coding sequences were highly conserved among contrasting cultivars, implying expression regulation rather than protein-coding variation underlies phenotype; molecular marker at MdMYB10 locus reportedly co-segregates with red-flesh phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transgenic apple and transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: EST database mining; qPCR expression profiling; metabolite profiling by HPLC and LC-MS

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Samsun; Red Field; Pacific RoseTM; Royal Gala
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tobacco leaves when co-expressed with MdMYB10; apple fruit skin/cortex context inferred
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances MdMYB10-mediated anthocyanin accumulation and strongly boosts DFR promoter activation in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH IIIf clade; putative TT8 homologue; MYB-interaction N-terminal motifs present
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Co-activation with MYB partner to activate anthocyanin gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent cofactor that strengthens MdMYB10 activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis, but not shown to be the main limiting determinant in apple
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR promoter in conjunction with MdMYB10
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances MdMYB10-dependent activation of DFR promoter; co-expression intensifies and accelerates anthocyanin production
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms functional MYB-bHLH regulatory complex with MdMYB10
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Relatively consistent expression through apple fruit development and between cultivars; does not correlate strongly with pigment levels
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves; assayed in developing apple fruit tissues and transgenic apple
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium infiltration; fruit developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; dual-luciferase assay; transient tobacco pigmentation assay; qPCR expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdbHLH3 is an apple IIIf-clade bHLH candidate with motifs consistent with MYB interaction. 2. Co-expression with MdMYB10 gives the strongest activation of the Arabidopsis DFR promoter in tobacco transient assays. 3. Co-infiltration with MdMYB10 accelerates and intensifies anthocyanin accumulation in tobacco leaves. 4. Endogenous expression in apple does not track anthocyanin accumulation, suggesting it is not the principal limiting regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive cofactor for anthocyanin engineering when stacked with MdMYB10
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No evidence that MdMYB10 up-regulates MdbHLH3 transcript levels in apple transgenics
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: EST database mining; qPCR expression profiling; metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-MS

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Samsun; Red Field; Pacific RoseTM; Royal Gala
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tobacco leaves when co-expressed with MdMYB10; apple fruit skin/cortex context inferred
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances MdMYB10-mediated activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis in transient assays, though less strongly than MdbHLH3
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH IIIf clade; putative Delila homologue; MYB-interaction N-terminal motifs present
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Co-activation with MYB partner to activate anthocyanin gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive cofactor for MdMYB10 activity, not shown as primary determinant of anthocyanin level in apple
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR promoter in conjunction with MdMYB10
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms functional MYB-bHLH regulatory complex with MdMYB10
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Relatively consistent across development and cultivars; not correlated with anthocyanin accumulation pattern
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves; assayed in developing apple fruit tissues and transgenic apple
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium infiltration; fruit developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; dual-luciferase assay; qPCR expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdbHLH33 is an apple IIIf-clade bHLH candidate with conserved MYB-interaction motifs. 2. It can activate the DFR promoter together with MdMYB10 in tobacco transient assays. 3. Its expression pattern in apple fruit does not mirror anthocyanin accumulation, indicating it is not the main regulatory switch.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary cofactor for anthocyanin engineering together with MdMYB10
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Effect in transient assay was weaker than that of MdbHLH3; no evidence of transcriptional induction by MdMYB10 in transgenic apple
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: EST database mining; qPCR expression profiling
